verses.sanskrit: एतदाख्याय तत्सर्वं हनुमान् मारुतात्मजः ।भूयः समुपचक्राम वचनं वक्तुमुत्तरम् ।। ५.५९.१ ।।।।
verses.text:
After informing all this, Hanuma the son of wind-god started telling more of the following 
words.

verses.sanskrit: सफलो राघवोद्योगः सुग्रीवस्य च संभ्रमः ।शीलमासाद्य सीताया मम च प्रवणं मनः ।। ५.५९.२ ।।।।
verses.text:
"By the power of virtuousness of Seetha, Rama's effort and Sugreeva's zeal are getting 
fulfilled. My mind too is devoted to her."

verses.sanskrit: तपसा धारयेल्लोकान् क्रुद्धो वा निर्दहेदपि ।सर्वथा ऽतिप्रवृद्धो ऽसौ रावणो राक्षसाधिपः ।तस्य तां स्पृशतो गात्रं तपसा न विनाशितम् ।। ५.५९.३ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Ravana, the king of demons, who is strong in all ways, can burn away the three worlds 
by the power of his penance. Even if he is enraged, he can scorch away his adversaries."

verses.sanskrit: न तदग्निशिखा कुर्यात् संस्पृष्टा पाणिना सती ।जनकस्यात्मजा कुर्याद्यत् क्रोधकलुषीकृता ।। ५.५९.४ ।।।।
verses.text:
"His body, which touched Seetha the daughter of Janaka, was not destroyed because of 
his askesis. Even a flame, when fully touched with one's hand, cannot do that which Seetha would, if 
stirred by anger."

verses.sanskrit: जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् सर्वाननुज्ञाप्य महाहरीन् ।अस्मिन्नेवंगते कार्ये भवतां च निवेदिते ।। ५.५९.५ ।।।।
verses.text:
"This work (of mine in the form of Seetha's discovery) has been informed to you in this 
way. It is indeed proper to see those two princes (Rama and Lakshmana) along with Seetha, after taking 
permission from all the great monkeys headed by Jambavan.

verses.sanskrit: न्याय्यं स्म सह वैदेह्या द्रष्टुं तौ पार्थिवात्मजौ ।। ५.५९.६ ।।।।
अहमेको ऽपि पर्याप्तः सराक्षसगणां पुरीम् ।तां लङ्कां तपसा हन्तुं रावणं च महाबलम् ।। ५.५९.७ ।।।।
verses.text:
"I am sufficient, even alone; to destroy rapidly that City of Lanka together with those 
troops of demons as also the mighty Ravana."

verses.sanskrit: किं पुनः सहितो वीरैर्बलवद्भिः कृतात्मभिः ।कृतास्त्रैः प्लवगैः शूरैर्भवद्भिर्विजयैषिभिः ।। ५.५९.८ ।।।।
verses.text:
"When accompanied by you, who are strong, whose spirit is disciplined, who are proficient 
in the use of arms, valiant, longing for victory and moving by jumps, how much more is required?"

verses.sanskrit: अहं तु रावणं युद्धे ससैन्यं सपुरस्सरम् ।सहपुत्रं वधिष्यामि सहोदरयुतं युधि ।। ५.५९.९ ।।।।
verses.text:
"In the battle, I on my part will kill Ravana along with his army, his forerunners, his 
sons and his brothers."

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्ममैन्द्रं च रौद्रं च वायव्यं वारुणं तथा ।यदि शक्रजितो ऽस्त्राणि दुर्निरीक्षाणि संयुगे ।। ५.५९.१० ।।।।
verses.text:
"In the battle, I can blow out the missiles presided over by Indra the lord of celestials, 
Brahma the creator, Rudra the god of destruction, Vayu the wind-god, Varuna the god of water and even 
those missiles even if they are difficult to be looked at, employed by Indrajit (Ravana's eldest son).

verses.sanskrit: तान्यहं विधामिष्यामि निहनिष्यामि राक्षसान् ।भवतामभ्यनुज्ञातो विक्रमो मे रुणाद्धि तम् ।। ५.५९.११ ।।।।
verses.text:
"My prowess, when permitted by you, will frustrate that Ravana. The shower of rocks, 
which will be unequalled and incessant, discharged by me, can kill even celestials. How much more those 
demons?"

verses.sanskrit: मया ऽतुला विसृष्टा हि शैलवृष्टिर्निरन्तरा ।देवानपि रणे हन्यात् किं पुनस्तान्निशाचरान् ।। ५.५९.१२ ।।।।
सागरो ऽप्यतियाद्वेलां मन्दरः प्रचलेदपि ।न जाम्बवन्तं समरे कम्पयेदरिवाहिनी ।। ५.५९.१३ ।।।।
verses.text:
"The ocean may overflow its shore. Mount Mandara may move from its position. But, in 
battle, no army of adversaries can stagger Jambavan."

verses.sanskrit: सर्वराक्षससङ्घानां राक्षसा ये च पूर्वकाः ।अलमेको विनाशाय वीरो वालिसुतः कपिः ।। ५.५९.१४ ।।।।
verses.text:
"The heroic Angada, the son of Vali alone, is sufficient to destroy all the troops of 
demons and even the demons who were their progenitors."

verses.sanskrit: पनसस्योरुवेगेन नीलस्य च महात्मनः ।मन्दरो ऽप्यवशीर्येत किं पुनर्युधि राक्षसाः ।। ५.५९.१५ ।।।।
verses.text:
"By the rapid movement of the thighs of the high-souled Neela and of Panasa, even Mount 
Mandara can be shattered. How much more, the demons in battle?"

verses.sanskrit: सदेवासुरयक्षेषु गन्धर्वोरगपक्षिषु ।मैन्दस्य प्रतियोद्धारं शंसत द्विविदस्य वा ।। ५.५९.१६ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Tell me any one who can fight against Mainda or Dvivida, among Gandharvas the celestial 
singers or Uragas the serpents or Pakshis the birds or the celestials or the demons or Yakshas the semi-divine 
beings."

verses.sanskrit: अश्विपुत्रौ महाभागावेतौ प्लवगसत्तमौ ।एतयोः प्रतियोद्धारं न पश्यामि रणाजिरे ।। ५.५९.१७ ।।।।
verses.text:
"These two illustrious sons of Ashvini Kumaras, Mainda and Dvivida are the foremost among 
the monkeys. In the battle-field, I do not find anyone who can fight against these two monkeys."

verses.sanskrit: पितामहवरोत्सेकात् परमं दर्पमास्थितौ ।अमृतप्राशिनावेतौ सर्ववानरसत्तमौ ।। ५.५९.१८ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Proud of having received boons from Brahma the creator and their grandfather and abiding 
in a supreme haughtiness, these two foremost among all the monkeys live on Amrita the nectar."

verses.sanskrit: अश्विनोर्माननार्थं हि सर्वलोकपितामहः ।सर्वावध्यत्वमतुलमनयोर्दत्तवान् पुरा ।। ५.५९.१९ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Long ago, Brahma the grandfather of all the worlds gave an unequal boon to them in honour 
of Ashvini Kumaras regarding their inviolability."

verses.sanskrit: करोत्सेकेन मुक्तौ च प्रमथ्य महतीं चमूम् ।सुराणाममृतं वीरौ पीतवन्तौ प्लवङ्गमौ ।। ५.५९.२० ।।।।
verses.text:
"By the pride of those boons, those two heroic monkeys harassed a great army of celestials 
and thereupon drank up nectar, excited as they were with joy."

verses.sanskrit: एतावेव हि संक्रुद्धौ सवाजिरथकुञ्जराम् ।लङ्कां नाशयितुं शक्तौ सर्वे तिष्ठन्तु वानराः ।। ५.५९.२१ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Let all the other monkeys stand aside. Even these Mainda and Dvivida on their part are 
capable of destroying the entire city of Lanka together with its horses, chariots and elephants."

verses.sanskrit: मयैव निहता लङ्कां दग्धा भस्मीकृता पुनः ।राजमार्गेषु सर्वत्र नाम विश्रावितं मया ।। ५.५९.२२ ।।।।
verses.text:
"I alone burnt and destroyed the City of Lanka. In all its royal high-ways, the name 
(of Rama and others) was proclaimed by me as follows:

verses.sanskrit: जयत्यतिबलो रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः ।राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः ।। ५.५९.२३ ।।।।
verses.text:
"'Victorious is Rama, the exceedingly strong and the mighty Lakshmana too! Victorious 
is King Sugreeva, protected by Rama. I am Hanuma, a servant of Rama and the son of wind-god'. In this 
way, the name (of Rama and others) was heralded by me everywhere."

verses.sanskrit: अहं कोसलराजस्य दासः पवनसम्भवः ।हनुमानिति सर्वत्र नाम विश्रावितं मया ।। ५.५९.२४ ।।।।
अशोकवनिकामध्ये रावणस्य दुरात्मनः ।अधस्ताच्छिंशुपावृक्षे साध्वी करुणमास्थिता ।। ५.५९.२५ ।।।।
verses.text:
"In the midst of Ashoka garden of the evil-minded Ravana and underneath a Shimshupa tree, 
the virtuous Seetha was seen staying dejected, encircled as she was with female-demons, agonized with 
grief and anguish, lusterless like a digit of the moon encircled by a line of clouds and disregarding 
that Ravana who is arrogant of his strength."

verses.sanskrit: राक्षसीभिः परिवृता शोकसन्तापकर्शिता ।मेघलेखापरिवृता चन्द्रलेखेव निष्प्रभा ।अचिन्तयन्ती वैदेही रावणं बलदर्पितम् ।। ५.५९.२६ ।।।।
पतिव्रता च सुश्रोणी अवष्टब्धा च जानकी ।। ५.५९.२७ ।।
verses.text:
"Seetha, a devoted and virtuous wife, having charming hips, has been placed under restraint. 
The auspicious Seetha is devoted to Rama in all ways, giving her undivided thought to Rama like Shachi 
the goddess, giving her concern to Indra the destroyer of strong holds."

verses.sanskrit: वृता चन्द्रलेखेव निष्प्रभा ।अनुरक्ता हि वैदेही रामं सर्वात्मना शुभा ।अनन्यचित्ता रामे च पौलोमीव पुरन्दरे ।। ५.५९.२८ ।।।।
तदेकवासः संवीता रजोध्वस्ता तथैव च ।शोकसन्तापदीनाङ्गी सीता भर्तृहिते रता ।। ५.५९.२९ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Weaving that single piece of cloth, which she had on her person even while being borne 
away by Ravana, and likewise covered with dust, she was seen by me in a miserable condition with her 
grief and anguish, devoted as she was to the welfare of her husband."

verses.sanskrit: सा मया राक्षसीमध्ये तर्ज्यमाना मुहुर्मुहुः ।राक्षसीभिर्विरूपाभिर्दृष्टा हि प्रमदावने ।। ५.५९.३० ।।।।
verses.text:
"I saw Seetha in a royal pleasure garden, amidst some female-demons. Seetha was being 
frightened time and again by those ugly female-demons. That Seetha, wearing a single braid of hair and 
looking depressed, was fully engaged in thinking about her lord. Looking like a lotus with a discoloured 
figure in winter, she had determined to die, utterly averting to do anything with Ravana."

verses.sanskrit: एकवेणीधरा दीना भर्तृचिन्तापरायणा ।अधः शय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे ।। ५.५९.३१ ।।।।
रावणाद् विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया ।कथंचिन्मृगशावाक्षी विश्वासमुपपादिता ।। ५.५९.३२ ।।।।
verses.text:
"With great difficulty, I created confidence in that fawn-eyed Seetha. Thereafter, I 
talked to her and explained everything to her. She was delighted to hear the companionship cropped up 
between Rama and Sugreeva."

verses.sanskrit: ततः सम्भाषिता चैव सर्वमर्थं च दर्शिता ।रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च श्रुत्वा प्रीतिमुपागता ।। ५.५९.३३ ।।।।
नियतः समुदाचारो भक्तिर्भर्तरि चोत्तमा ।यन्न हन्ति दशग्रीवं स महात्मा कृतागसम् ।निमित्तमात्रं रामस्तु वधे तस्य भविष्यति ।। ५.५९.३४ ।।।।
verses.text:
"That high-souled Seetha does not kill Ravana who has committed an offence because she 
conducts herself well with restraint and since supreme is her devotion to her lord."

verses.sanskrit: सा प्रकृत्यैव तन्वङ्गी तद्वियोगाच्च कर्शिता ।प्रतिपत्पाठशीलस्य विद्येव तनुतां गता ।। ५.५९.३५ ।।।।
verses.text:
"Rama, on his part, will become mere instrument in killing that Ravana. That Seetha, 
who is by her own nature, on delicate-limbed woman, was emaciated because of separation from her lord. 
She had grown thin like the learning of a scholar continuing his studies even on the first day of lunar 
fortnight (which must be observed as a complete holiday by students."

verses.sanskrit: एवमास्ते महाभागा सीता शोकपरायणा ।यदत्र प्रतिकर्तव्यं तत्सर्वमुपपद्यताम् ।। ५.५९.३६ ।।
verses.text:
"Thus, the illustrious Seetha remains immersed in grief. Let all that be done, which 
requires to be counteracted by way of a relief in this matter."
